{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-40944", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.0, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:U"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-295", "name": "Improper Certificate Validation"}], "description": "### Summary
The `trustedCertPool()` function in the TLS configuration only parses the first PEM block from CA certificate files. When a CA bundle contains multiple certificates (e.g., intermediate + root CA), only the first certificate is loaded. This silently breaks certificate chain validation for mTLS.

### Impact
In deployments using mTLS with certificate chains (intermediate CA + root CA bundles), legitimate clients with properly chained certificates are rejected with `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority`. This degrades the security posture by making mTLS unusable with standard CA chain configurations, potentially forcing operators to disable client certificate verification.

All versions using TLS with `trustedCaFile` configuration are affected.

### Details
In `common/security/tls.go`, the `trustedCertPool()` method calls `pem.Decode()` only once, processing a single PEM block. The remaining bytes (containing additional certificates) are silently discarded. Additionally, the error return from `pem.Decode` is ignored, so a corrupted CA file results in an empty certificate pool without any error.

### Patches
Fixed by iterating over all PEM blocks in the file, parsing each CERTIFICATE block, and returning an error if no valid certificates are found.

### Workarounds
Use CA files containing only a single certificate (the direct issuer of client certificates, not a chain).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0016, "percentile": 0.05558}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-14T23:15:16Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-40944"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-21T22:16:19Z", "published_at": "2026-04-14T23:15:16Z", "references": ["https://github.com/oxia-db/oxia/security/advisories/GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-40944", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/oxia-db/oxia/security-advisories/GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/oxia-db/oxia", "summary": "Oxia's TLS CA certificate chain validation fails with multi-certificate PEM bundles", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T20:39:59Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.16.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/oxia-db/oxia"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.16.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Oxia's TLS CA certificate chain validation fails with multi-certificate PEM bundles
